2605.02730.title: Perceptual Flow Network for Visually Grounded Reasoning
2605.02730.paper_summary: Despite the success of Large-Vision Language Models (LVLMs), general optimization objectives (e.g., standard MLE) fail to constrain visual trajectories, leading to language bias and hallucination. To mitigate this, current methods introduce geometric priors from visual experts as additional supervision. However, we observe that such supervision is typically suboptimal: it is biased toward geometric precision and offers limited reasoning utility. To bridge this gap, we propose Perceptual Flow Network (PFlowNet), which eschews rigid alignment with the expert priors and achieves interpretable yet more effective visual reasoning. Specifically, PFlowNet decouples perception from reasoning to establish a self-conditioned generation process. Based on this, it integrates multi-dimensional rewards with vicinal geometric shaping via variational reinforcement learning, thereby facilitating reasoning-oriented perceptual behaviors while preserving visual reliability. PFlowNet delivers a provable performance guarantee and competitive empirical results, particularly setting new SOTA records on V* Bench (90.6%) and MME-RealWorld-lite (67.0%).
2605.02730.paper_summary_zh: 儘管大型視覺語言模型（LVLMs）取得了成功，但一般優化目標（例如，標準最大似然估計）未能約束視覺軌跡，導致語言偏見和幻覺。為了減輕這一問題，目前的方法引入了來自視覺專家的幾何先驗作為額外的監督。然而，我們觀察到這種監督通常是次優的：它偏向於幾何精確性，並提供有限的推理效用。為了彌補這一差距，我們提出了感知流網絡（PFlowNet），該網絡避免與專家先驗的僵化對齊，並實現可解釋但更有效的視覺推理。具體而言，PFlowNet 將感知與推理解耦，以建立自我條件生成過程。基於此，它通過變分強化學習整合多維獎勵與鄰近幾何塑形，從而促進以推理為導向的感知行為，同時保持視覺可靠性。PFlowNet 提供了可證明的性能保證和具有競爭力的實證結果，特別是在 V* Bench（90.6%）和 MME-RealWorld-lite（67.0%）上創造了新的 SOTA 記錄。
2605.02730.author: Yangfu Li et.al.
2605.02730.authors: Yangfu Li, Yuning Gong, Hongjian Zhan, Teng Li, Yuanhuiyi Lyu, Tianyi Chen, Qi Liu, Ziyuan Huang, Zhihang Zhong, Dandan Zheng, Yue Lu
